September 10, 1976 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804 
Gentlemen: 
This administration has a question on the "Conflict of 
Interest Code" that needs answering. 
The situation lS 
as follows: 
The Office of Merced County Superintendent of Schools is 
the Local Educational Agency for the Regional Migrant 
Education Program. 
This program covers five (5) counties 
which include Madera, Merced, Stanislaus, San Joaquin, 
and Contra Costa Counties. 
The Office of Merced County 
Superintendent of Schools is responsible for the employ-
ment, instruction, and fiscal aspects of the program. 
QUESTION: 
Since the Regional Migrant Program covers five 
counties and is but a fraction of the County Schools 
operation, will it be necessary for this office to submit 
the "Conflict of Interest Code" adopted by the County 
Board of Education to the Fair Political Practices Commission 
or should it be submitted to our County Board of Supervisors. 
Please advise. 
JLB: jp 
Sincerely, 
~f.~=-
OCKL.~~ 
Assistant County Superintendent, 
Business Administration

sections.question:
Since the Regional Migrant Program covers five 
counties and is but a fraction of the County Schools 
operation, will it be necessary for this office to submit 
the "Conflict of Interest Code" adopted by the County 
Board of Education to the Fair Political Practices Commission 
or should it be submitted to our County Board of Supervisors. 
Please advise. 
JLB: jp
sections.conclusion: It is our conclusion that, pursuant to the provisions of Public Law 93-380, the Merced County Office of Education has been designated by the State Board of Education as the local educational agency (LEA) to administer the program in your region. As a local agency within the meaning of the Political Reform Act (Government Code Section 82111 et seq.), the Office of the County Superintendent of Schools is subject to the code reviewing procedures of the County Board of Supervisors.
sections.question_synthetic: Should the Merced County Office of the Superintendent of Schools, which administers a regional migrant education program covering five counties, submit its Conflict of Interest Code to the FPPC or to the County Board of Supervisors?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Merced County Office of Education, as the designated local educational agency (LEA), is subject to code review procedures under the County Board of Supervisors rather than needing separate FPPC submission.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document contains OCR artifacts and formatting issues that impacted clarity of some text. The question and conclusion sections were reconstructed from partially damaged source material. The second page contains the incoming request dated September 10, 1976, and the first page contains the FPPC response dated October 25, 1976.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Since the Regional Migrant Program covers five 
counties and is but a fraction of the County Schools 
operation, will it be necessary for this office to submit 
the "Conflict of Interest Code" adopted by the County 
Board of Education to the Fair Political Practices Commission 
or should it be submitted to our County Board of Supervisors. 
Please advise. 
JLB: jp

CONCLUSION: It is our conclusion that, pursuant to the provisions of Public Law 93-380, the Merced County Office of Education has been designated by the State Board of Education as the local educational agency (LEA) to administer the program in your region. As a local agency within the meaning of the Political Reform Act (Government Code Section 82111 et seq.), the Office of the County Superintendent of Schools is subject to the code reviewing procedures of the County Board of Supervisors.

The situation lS as follows: The Office of Merced County Superintendent of Schools is the Local Educational Agency for the Regional Migrant Education Program. This program covers five (5) counties which include Madera, Merced, Stanislaus, San Joaquin, and Contra Costa Counties. The Office of Merced County Superintendent of Schools is responsible for the employ- ment, instruction, and fiscal aspects of the program. QUESTION: Since the Regional Migrant Program covers five counties and is but a fraction of the County Schools operation, will it be necessary for this office to submit the "Conflict of Interest Code" adopted by the County Board of Education to the Fair Political Practices Commission or should it be submitted to our County Board of Supervisors. embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that the Merced County Superintendent of Schools, designated as the Local Educational Agency for a five-county Regional Migrant Education Program, should submit its Conflict of Interest Code to the County Board of Supervisors for review rather than directly to the FPPC. The determination is based on the office's status as a local agency under the Political Reform Act.
